summary.intent: Upgrade the whatsapp-rust ecosystem from 0.2 to 0.5 to fix inbound WhatsApp messages being parsed as empty (GitHub #534), then add WhatsApp to the default offered channels list, resolve PR review comments, and fix a QR code display bug during onboarding.
summary.outcome: All 6 whatsapp crates bumped to 0.5, stores updated with 11 new trait methods, WhatsApp added to default channels, QR code polling fallback added, duplicate account start guard added, and PR #709 created with all review threads resolved.

repo: wacore and wacore-binary 0.5 default features include 'simd' which requires unstable core::simd::Select — must set default-features = false on both to avoid nightly incompatibility
whatsapp-rust 0.5 requires a new 'tokio-runtime' feature flag and .with_runtime(TokioRuntime) in the bot builder (new 4th type parameter)

There are two separate default channel lists: config/schema/runtime.rs (authoritative) and gateway/state.rs (hardcoded fallback before config loads), plus JS fallbacks in onboarding-view.js and page-channels.js

WhatsApp QR codes are delivered exclusively via WebSocket events with drop_if_slow: true — no REST polling fallback existed, causing QR to silently disappear if event fired before frontend subscription was ready
Stored channels from moltis.db are auto-started on gateway boot; re-adding an already-running account via onboarding caused a sled lock error (EAGAIN) rather than being handled gracefully

ProtocolStore in 0.5 adds 8 new methods: get_tc_token, put_tc_token, delete_tc_token, get_all_tc_token_jids, delete_expired_tc_tokens, store_sent_message, take_sent_message, delete_expired_sent_messages
SKDM methods changed signature from String to Jid type in 0.5

code.finding: health_snapshot() exposes latest_qr via ChannelHealthSnapshot.extra so QR code is pollable via channels.status RPC — added as fallback for missed WebSocket events
start_account() now checks account_ids() before starting — if already running, returns Ok(()) to handle onboarding re-add gracefully
default_channels_offered() is the authoritative default list for channel picker
Stored channels from moltis.db are restored and started here on gateway boot — separate from config-file channels
Added 2s polling fallback using channels.status RPC to fetch QR code when WebSocket event is missed; polling stops once QR obtained or modal closed
get_latest_sync_key_id() requires explicit tracking via Arc<Mutex<Option<Vec<u8>>>> field — DashMap iteration order is non-deterministic so cannot rely on it for 'latest'

workflow: After version bumps, disable default-features on crates with feature flags that require unstable Rust before doing cargo check
cargo check -p <specific-crate> is faster than workspace-wide check when iterating on trait implementation errors
just test with --all-features pulls in CUDA deps that fail on macOS; use nextest without all-features for the workspace test run
PR review comments should be replied to via gh api and threads resolved via graphql mutation resolveReviewThread
Use time::OffsetDateTime::now_utc().unix_timestamp() instead of manual SystemTime epoch math for timestamp generation

friction: wacore/wacore-binary 0.5 default 'simd' feature breaks compilation on the pinned nightly — required two separate default-features = false edits discovered iteratively
Cargo.lock merge conflict after merging main — resolved by taking main's lockfile and re-running cargo fetch
Push was blocked by rebase divergence requiring stash/pull/pop dance
Background task notifications for already-completed tasks created confusion about push status
No REST endpoint existed for QR codes — required adding health snapshot exposure + JS polling as a workaround for a WebSocket reliability issue
Sled lock error surface message was opaque — root cause (duplicate start from DB restore + onboarding re-add) required tracing through init_channels.rs and channel.rs
open_items: Users with existing WhatsApp pairings (0.2 sled data) may need to re-pair (re-scan QR) if wacore struct serialization changed — should be documented in PR and release notes
Manual QA verification still needed: pair a WhatsApp device, send a text DM, verify it routes to LLM and produces a reply
The double path .moltis/whatsapp/whatsapp/<account_id> looks redundant — worth cleaning up in a future refactor
WhatsApp as default offered channel may need feature-flag gating if WhatsApp support isn't available in all deployment configurations
